sources.title: AI Agent Adoption 2026: What the Data Shows
claims: 31% of enterprises have AI agents in production
40% of enterprise apps expected to include task-specific agents by end of 2026 (up from <5% in 2025)
Banking and insurance lead adoption at 47%; healthcare and government trail at 18% and 14%
80% of enterprise apps shipped or updated in Q1 2026 embed at least one AI agent, up from 33% in 2024
Median time-to-value on agent deployments is 5.1 months; SDR agents pay back in 3.4 months; finance/ops in 8.9 months
By end of 2027, 40%+ of agentic AI projects forecast to stall due to rising costs, unclear value, insufficient risk controls

sources.title: AI Agents Statistics 2026: Adoption, Market Growth and Key Trends
claims: Global market for AI agents projected from $5.29B (2024) to $216.8B by 2035
35% of organizations report broad agent usage; 27% limited experiments; 17% org-wide deployment
62% of organizations experiment with AI agents; fewer than 25% have scaled to production

sources.title: Chinese AI startup Manus reportedly gets funding from Benchmark at $500M valuation
claims: Manus raised $75M Series B from Benchmark at ~$500M valuation
Manus launch date: March 6, 2025
Waitlist hit 2 million people within one week of launch

sources.title: Manus at $90M/year — Revenue and Growth Data
claims: Manus reached $90M in annualized revenue by August 2025 — five months after launch

sources.title: Meta acquires intelligent agent firm Manus, capping year of aggressive AI moves
claims: Meta acquired Manus for a reported $2–3 billion in December 2025
Largest AI computer use agent acquisition on record
Manus continues operating its subscription service post-acquisition

sources.title: Anthropic says Claude can now use your computer to finish tasks for you
claims: Anthropic made Claude Computer Use generally available to all enterprise customers on March 24, 2026
One in five U.S. businesses now subscribes to Anthropic, up from one in twenty-five a year prior

sources.title: OSWorld Benchmark 2026 — 82% vs 38%: Why Your Computer Use Agent Choice Matters
claims: Human baseline on OSWorld: 72.36%
OSWorld 2026 leaderboard: Coasty 82%, Claude Sonnet 4.6 73%, Simular Agent S2 72.6%, GPT-5.3 Codex 65%, OpenAI Operator 38%
OSWorld consists of 369 real desktop tasks across real operating systems and real apps
sources.verified: partial — Coasty's own score is self-reported; OSWorld methodology verified via o-mega.ai

sources.title: 2025–2026 AI Computer-Use Benchmarks & Top AI Agents Guide
claims: OpenAI Operator scored 38.1% on OSWorld at launch (January 2025), described as 'state of the art' despite being below human baseline
OSWorld is the gold-standard benchmark for computer use AI, testing real desktop environments with no API wrappers

sources.title: Anthropic AI Statistics 2026: Users, Revenue & Market Share
claims: Anthropic ARR: ~$4B early 2025 → $14B February 2026 (Series G) → $30B April 2026
Anthropic closed $65B Series H in May 2026 at $965B post-money valuation
Anthropic overtook OpenAI to become the world's most valuable private AI startup

sources.title: AI agents arrived in 2025 — here's what happened and the challenges ahead in 2026
claims: Anthropic's Model Context Protocol (MCP) released late 2024 as a standardized tool-connection layer for AI agents
Google's Agent2Agent (A2A) protocol released April 2025, enabling cross-provider agent collaboration
MCP and A2A designed to work together, forming open multi-agent infrastructure

sources.title: Top 10 Privacy, AI & Cybersecurity Issues for 2026
claims: AI agents introduce novel privacy risks: they actively collect and use data across contexts rather than merely reproducing it
Risk of inappropriate context transfer — information appropriate in one setting transferred to another
2026 state AI bills expanding liability for AI-caused harms in employment and recruiting decisions

sources.title: Big Tech is about to spend $700 billion on AI this year
claims: Big Tech on track to spend $700B on AI infrastructure in 2026 — a significant fraction powering agentic workloads

unverified_flags: search_volume (~500,000/mo) is an author estimate for the combined AI agents/computer use category — not sourced from Ahrefs or Semrush
Market share percentages for Anthropic, OpenAI, Google, and Meta/Manus in the computer use agents sub-category specifically are [unverified] — no third-party breakdown found
Coasty's 82% OSWorld score is self-reported by Coasty; no independent third-party validation found as of June 2026
Qualcomm's talks to acquire Tenstorrent for $8–10B were reported as 'early stage' in May 2026; deal not confirmed closed as of research date
